item.title: Merge Forward Meeting - 2025-08-13
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded: Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon India in Hyderabad (August 6-7), and KubeCon + CloudNativeCon North America in Atlanta (November 10-13). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io

item.title: Deep Roots Meeting - 2025-08-12
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded: Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon India in Hyderabad (August 6-7), and KubeCon + CloudNativeCon North America in Atlanta (November 10-13). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io

item.title: Balancing Coupling in Software Design • Vlad Khononov & Sheen Brisals
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This interview was recorded for the GOTO Book Club. http://gotopia.tech/bookclub
Read the full transcription of the interview here (https://gotopia.tech/episodes/367) Sheen Brisals - AWS Serverless Hero, Engineering Leader & Co-Author of "Serverless Development on AWS" Vlad Khononov - Author of "Balancing Coupling in Software Design" & "Learning Domain Driven Design" & Creator of the Balanced Coupling Model
RESOURCES Vlad https://bsky.app/profile/vladikk.bsky.social https://vladikk.com https://github.com/vladikk https://www.linkedin.com/in/vladikk
Sheen https://bsky.app/profile/sheenbrisals.bsky.social https://twitter.com/sheenbrisals https://www.linkedin.com/in/sheen-brisals https://sbrisals.medium.com
Links https://www.informit.com https://youtu.be/6hTZXR2brWE
DESCRIPTION Sheen Brisals sits down with software engineer and author Vlad Khononov to explore his latest book, "Balancing Coupling in Software Design". Vlad shares his journey from a failed microservices project to his deep dive into the principles of coupling, drawing insights from a 1970s structured design book.
The duo discusses the timeless nature of coupling in software, how modern systems like microservices and cloud architectures still rely on age-old design principles, and the importance of understanding complexity for better problem decomposition and estimation. Vlad also reveals his unique approach to the book—integrating AI-generated poetry into each chapter to help readers grasp complex concepts. With a focus on modularity as the antidote to complexity, Vlad emphasizes that by managing coupling, engineers can create more maintainable, scalable systems.
RECOMMENDED BOOKS Vlad Khononov • Balancing Coupling in Software Design (https://amzn.to/4iGpbDv) Vlad Khononov • Learning Domain-Driven Design (https://amzn.to/41EVhIX) Sheen Brisals & Luke Hedger • Serverless Development on AWS (https://amzn.to/3W3Sw2f) Glenford Myers • Composite/Structured Design (https://amzn.to/4kVblPN) Vaughn Vernon • Implementing Domain-Driven Design (https://amzn.to/41Mpk2E) Eric Evans • Domain-Driven Design (https://amzn.to/43lNxhv) van Kelle, Verschatse & Baas-Schwegler • Collaborative Software Design (https://amzn.to/4iv0N8I) Nick Tune & Jean-Georges Perrin • Architecture Moderniza (https://amzn.to/4b5ASiN)
Bluesky (https://bsky.app/profile/gotocon.com) Twitter (https://twitter.com/GOTOcon) Instagram (https://www.instagram.com/goto_con) LinkedIn (https://www.linkedin.com/company/goto-) Facebook (https://www.facebook.com/GOTOConferences)
CHANNEL MEMBERSHIP BONUS Join this channel to get early access to videos & other perks: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCs_tLP3AiwYKwdUHpltJPuA/join
Looking for a unique learning experience? Attend the next GOTO conference near you! Get your ticket: gotopia.tech (https://gotopia.tech)
SUBSCRIBE TO OUR YOUTUBE CHANNEL (https://www.youtube.com/user/GotoConferences/?sub_confirmation=1) - new videos posted daily!

item.title: ChatLoopBackOff: Episode 65 (Kubean)
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded: Ready to simplify Kubernetes cluster management across hybrid or multi-cloud environments? In this Live demo, CNCF Ambassador Afzal Ansari explores Kubean, a CNCF project that brings an Ansible-powered, GitOps-friendly approach to Kubernetes operations. Kubean aims to streamline the full lifecycle management of Kubernetes clusters—provisioning, upgrading, scaling, and decommissioning—using declarative configurations and a plugin-based architecture. Afzal will walk through the project’s core components, check out its integration with tools like Cluster API and OpenKruise, and assess its community momentum and production readiness. If you're interested in scalable cluster automation with a flexible control plane, don’t miss this first look at what Kubean has to offer.

item.title: DuckDB in 100 Seconds
item.author: Fireship
item.content:encoded:
Learn the basics of DuckDB - an in-process, analytical database optimized for OLAP workloads. It's lightweight, portable and requires no server setup, allowing it to excel at complex queries on structured data.
💬 Chat with Me on Discord
https://discord.gg/fireship
🔗 Resources
https://duckdb.org/
📚 Chapters
🔥 Get More Content - Upgrade to PRO
Upgrade at https://fireship.io/pro Use code YT25 for 25% off PRO access
🎨 My Editor Settings
- Atom One Dark - vscode-icons - Fira Code Font
🔖 Topics Covered - What is DuckDB? - Why DuckDB is great for analyzing massive datasets - Which companies use DuckDB? - How to run DuckDB - Useful DuckDB commands - Aggregations with time series data

item.title: Encrypted Computation: What if Decryption Wasn’t Needed? • Katharine Jarmul • GOTO 2024
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This presentation was recorded at GOTO Copenhagen 2024. #GOTOcon #GOTOcph https://gotocph.com
Katharine Jarmul - Principal Data Scientist at Thoughtworks & O'Reilly Author
RESOURCES https://twitter.com/kjam https://linkedin.com/in/katharinejarmul https://github.com/kjam https://kjamistan.com https://probablyprivate.com
Links https://www.tooltester.com/en/blog/chatgpt-statistics https://huggingface.co https://github.com/tf-encrypted/moose https://github.com/apple/swift-homomorphic-encryption https://github.com/zama-ai https://github.com/carbynestack
ABSTRACT If you are curious about the field of cryptography and what it has to offer data science and machine learning, this talk is for you!
We'll dive into the field of encrypted computation, where decryption isn't needed in order to perform calculations, transformations and operations on the data. You'll learn some of the core mathematical theory behind why and how this works, as well as the differences between approaches like homomorphic encryption and secure multi-party computation. At the end, you'll get some pointers and open-source library hints on where to go next and how to start using encrypted computation for problems you are solving the hard way (or not solving at all). [...]
TIMECODES 00:00 Intro 03:18 What if...? 04:23 Demo: Huggingface 06:25 Homomorphic encryption 08:41 Pallier cryptosystem 12:12 Demo: Pallier 15:22 Recap 19:55 Multi-party computation (MPC) 22:58 Use cases 29:54 Getting started with encrypted computation 32:45 Understanding builds trust 34:01 Outro
Download slides and read the full abstract here: https://gotocph.com/2024/sessions/3319
RECOMMENDED BOOKS Katharine Jarmul • Practical Data Privacy • https://amzn.to/3OafC3m Katharine Jarmul & Jacqueline Kazil • Data Wrangling with Python • https://amzn.to/3Ue5BV5 Katharine Jarmul & Richard Lawson • Python Web Scraping • https://amzn.to/44H7XPl
https://bsky.app/profile/gotocon.com https://twitter.com/GOTOcon https://www.linkedin.com/company/goto- https://www.instagram.com/goto_con https://www.facebook.com/GOTOConferences #Privacy #DataPrivacy #Huggingface #PrivacyTechnology #Swiftlang #Cryptography #Decryption #PrivacyEngineering #KatharineJarmul #CyberSecurity #Programming #SoftwareEngineering #HomomorphicEncryption #Encryption #TodayInTech
CHANNEL MEMBERSHIP BONUS Join this channel to get early access to videos & other perks: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCs_tLP3AiwYKwdUHpltJPuA/join
Looking for a unique learning experience? Attend the next GOTO conference near you! Get your ticket at https://gotopia.tech Sign up for updates and specials at https://gotopia.tech/newsletter
SUBSCRIBE TO OUR CHANNEL - new videos posted almost daily. https://www.youtube.com/user/GotoConferences/?sub_confirmation=1

item.title: CNCF On demand webinar: Troubleshooting Kubernetes with confidence
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded: Kubernetes debugging remains one of the most frustrating and time-consuming challenges in cloud native operations. When production issues hit, even seasoned platform engineers and SREs can find themselves grasping at logs, manually exec-ing into pods, and relying on shallow output from kubectl describe. In this conversational session, we sit down with Alban the creator of Traceloop and a core contributor to Inspektor Gadget, to explore how modern debugging is evolving, and what’s next. You’ll get a behind-the-scenes look at how Traceloop introduces kubectl-native tracing, powered by eBPF, to bring retroactive debugging into real-world Kubernetes clusters. Whether you're maintaining uptime, resolving incidents, or building internal tooling, this session will give you the practical insight and tools you need to troubleshoot Kubernetes with clarity and confidence.

item.title: My Smart Home Played me for a Fool
item.author: Jeff Geerling
item.content:encoded:
Sometimes smart devices make me feel really dumb. And dumb devices are a smart idea.
Mentioned in this video (some links are affiliate links):
- Home Assistant Connect ZWA-2: https://www.home-assistant.io/connect/zwa-2 - Zooz 800 Series Z-Wave Long Range Dimmer ZEN72: https://amzn.to/46NmRIj - HackRF One: https://amzn.to/40WKGK0
Thanks to Nabu Casa for sending the ZWA-2 for testing, and to Zooz for sending a few of their Z-Wave LR devices to connect to it. (The products used in this video were provided for review, but no money exchanged hands and they had no say in the video's contents.)
Support me on Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/geerlingguy Sponsor me on GitHub: https://github.com/sponsors/geerlingguy Merch: https://www.redshirtjeff.com 2nd Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@GeerlingEngineering 3rd Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@Level2Jeff
Contents:
00:00 - Smart TP 00:46 - My smart problem is dumb 01:43 - The fix 02:09 - Setting up ZWA-2 with Home Assistant 02:53 - A dumb switch that's smarter 04:20 - Making it smarter with Z-Wave LR 05:23 - How does it look on SDR? 06:48 - Spouse Approval Factor 07:09 - Should you buy one?

item.title: Why Gen Z Graduates Are in Crisis
item.author: ColdFusion
item.content:encoded:
Check out Odoo for free: https://www.odoo.com/r/OeAT
From the threat of AI taking jobs to a sluggish job market, Gen Z new graduates are being hit from all sides. But how did it get so bad and what's the solution? In this episode, we explore.
Watch or listen to ColdFusion on Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/1YEwCKoRz8fEDqheXB6UJ1
ColdFusion Music: https://www.youtube.com/@ColdFusionmusic http://burnwater.bandcamp.com
Sources and notes: https://docs.google.com/document/d/149g1HepLsX6TGy4-HB__NMObvGCsdOObPZ_Erywlew0/edit?usp=drivesdk
Get my book:
http://bit.ly/NewThinkingbook
ColdFusion Socials:
https://discord.gg/coldfusion https://facebook.com/ColdFusionTV https://twitter.com/ColdFusion_TV https://instagram.com/coldfusiontv
Created by: Dagogo Altraide Producers: Tawsif Akkas, Dagogo Altraide

item.title: Cloud Native Live - Kyverno 1.15: New Policy Types
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
Join us next week for a follow-up session where we explore the new policy types introduced in Kyverno 1.15 — MutatingPolicies, DeletingPolicies, and GeneratingPolicies.
This builds on our previous session, where we discussed Kyverno’s shift to CEL and introduced ValidatingPolicies and ImageValidatingPolicy. We’ll dive into how these new policies work, use cases they unlock, and how they fit into the future of Kubernetes policy management.

item.title: Developers Think "Testing" is Synonymous with "Unit Testing" – @garthgilmourni
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
Check out the full version on our YouTube channel now! #GOTOcon #SoftwareEngineering #SoftwareDevelopment #Testing #Stability #Complexity #Programming #UnitTesting #TDD #ProgrammingHistory #Coding #HistoryOfProgramming #ViralProgrammingShorts #Viral #ViralShorts #TodayInTech #GOTO
Full version available here: https://youtu.be/IlDIV5gaTP0
Garth Gilmour - Head Of Learning at Instil Software @garthgilmourni Eamonn Boyle - Development Manager at Gearset
RECOMMENDED BOOKS Gene Kim, Jez Humble, Nicole Forsgren, Patrick Debois & John Willis • The DevOps Handbook • https://amzn.to/3C0Rj0C Kiczales, des Rivieres & Bobrow • The Art of the Metaobject Protocol • https://amzn.to/3zbvavV Forsgren, Humble & Kim • Accelerate: The Science of Lean Software and DevOps • https://amzn.to/3tCz1xO Fred Brooks Jr. • The Mythical Man-Month • https://williamgibsonbooks.com Jeremy Kun • A Programmer's Introduction to Mathematics • https://amzn.to/3WbgKWz Scott Wlaschin • Domain Modeling Made Functional • https://amzn.to/3TPnLKz Tom Stuart • Understanding Computation • https://amzn.to/3Dt1Ymp Sam Halliday • Functional Programming for Mortals with Scalaz • https://leanpub.com/fpmortals Jack D. Hidary • Quantum Computing: An Applied Approach • https://amzn.to/3TAyk4g Daniel P. Friedman & Matthias Felleisen • The Little Schemer • https://amzn.to/3SzHNHI Matthew Skelton & Manuel Pais • Team Topologies • http://amzn.to/3sVLyLQ

item.title: Reading Code Effectively: An Overlooked Developer Skill • Marit van Dijk & Hannes Lowette
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This interview was recorded for GOTO Unscripted. https://gotopia.tech
Read the full transcription of this interview here (https://gotopia.tech/articles/366) Marit van Dijk - Developer Advocate at JetBrains, Java Champion & Open Source Contributor Hannes Lowette - Principal Consultant at Axxes, Monolith Advocate, Speaker & Whiskey Lover
RESOURCES Marit https://bsky.app/profile/maritvandijk.bsky.social https://linkedin.com/in/maritvandijk https://github.com/mlvandijk https://medium.com/@mlvandijk https://maritvandijk.com
Hannes https://bsky.app/profile/hanneslowette.net https://twitter.com/hannes_lowette https://github.com/Belenar https://linkedin.com/in/hanneslowette
Links https://www.felienne.com https://codereading.club https://github.com/neontribe/code-reading-club
DESCRIPTION Reading code is a critical yet often underappreciated skill in software development. Marit van Dijk & Hannes Lowette highlight that while developers are trained to write code, they spend most of their time understanding existing codebases—often with incomplete documentation and evolving complexity.
They discuss research-backed strategies, such as structured code reading exercises, participation in communities like the Code Reading Club, and leveraging modern IDE tools to navigate and comprehend unfamiliar code. The conversation underscores the importance of empathy in code reviews, writing clear commit messages, and using tests as documentation to improve collaboration and maintainability. By practicing code reading deliberately and utilizing available resources, developers can become more effective and adaptable in their work.
RECOMMENDED BOOKS Felienne Hermans • The Programmer's Brain (https://amzn.to/3mENbN0) Adrienne Braganza Tacke • "Looks Good to Me": Constructive Code Reviews (https://amzn.to/3E75XrD) Duncan McGregor & Nat Pryce • Java to Kotlin (https://amzn.to/4apSjLu) (https://amzn.to/40OIDa3) Saleem Siddiqui • Learning Test-Driven Development (https://amzn.to/35OMb3n) Roy Osherove • The Art of Unit Testing (https://bit.ly/3obiKNB) Trisha Gee & Helen Scott • Getting to Know IntelliJ IDEA (https://amzn.to/3ZBgnGc) Jacqui Read • Communication Patt (https://amzn.to/3E37lvv)
Bluesky (https://bsky.app/profile/gotocon.com) Twitter (https://twitter.com/GOTOcon) Instagram (https://www.instagram.com/goto_con) LinkedIn (https://www.linkedin.com/company/goto-) Facebook (https://www.facebook.com/GOTOConferences)
CHANNEL MEMBERSHIP BONUS Join this channel to get early access to videos & other perks: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCs_tLP3AiwYKwdUHpltJPuA/join

item.title: Introducing the data accessor of the EstimatorReport
item.author: probabl
item.content:encoded:
skore v0.10 is out! We added a data accessor to the EstimatorReport that allows you to interactively explore your dataset to gain insights.
------ Links:
skore v0.10 documentation: https://docs.skore.probabl.ai/0.10/index.html
skore GitHub repository: https://github.com/probabl-ai/skore
Website: https://probabl.ai/ LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/probabl Twitter: https://x.com/probabl_ai Bluesky: https://bsky.app/profile/probabl.bsky.social Discord: https://discord.probabl.ai
We also host a podcast called Sample Space, which you can find on your favourite podcast player. All the links can be found here: https://rss.com/podcasts/sample-space/
#probabl
